title: Gooey Filter
description: An SVG-based gooey filter component that applies a blob/liquid merging effect to child elements via a CSS filter reference. Paired with a pixel-trail cursor effect it produces melting, organic blob trails on mouse movement.
prompt_text:
# Gooey Filter An SVG-based gooey filter component that applies a blob/liquid merging effect to child elements via a CSS filter reference. Paired with a pixel-trail cursor effect it produces melting, organic blob trails on mouse movement. ## Use when - Building a creative portfolio or art-direction page with a fluid, organic cursor effect. - Adding a playful gooey merging animation to interactive UI elements. - Creating an immersive dark canvas where cursor movement leaves melting pixel trails. - Experimenting with SVG filter-based visual effects without heavy WebGL dependencies. ## Stack - Framework: react - Libraries: framer-motion, uuid - Requires: tailwind, shadcn ## Install ```bash framer-motion uuid ``` ## Component — gooey-filter.tsx ```tsx const GooeyFilter = ({ id = "goo-filter", strength = 10, }: { id?: string strength?: number }) => { return ( ) } export { GooeyFilter } ``` ## Demo ```tsx import { useScreenSize } from "@/hooks/use-screen-size" import { PixelTrail } from "@/components/ui/pixel-trail" import { GooeyFilter } from "@/components/ui/gooey-filter" function GooeyDemo() { const screenSize = useScreenSize() return (

Speaking things into existence

) } export { GooeyDemo } ``` ## Notes - The `GooeyFilter` component renders a hidden SVG that defines the filter. Apply it to any wrapper via `style={{ filter: 'url(#your-filter-id)' }}`. - The demo requires `PixelTrail` (framer-motion + uuid) and `useScreenSize` / `useDimensions` helper hooks — copy those from the source or implement equivalents. - Adjust `strength` to control blob merge radius: lower values (3–5) create subtle liquid edges; higher values (10+) create large blob merges.
